reviews.review_msg: Dr. Ahmadi is a caring and respected prof who is eager to respond to questions and pushes students to succeed. The lectures are heavily theory based and some subjects involve memorizing technical terms. The Solidworks labs and stapler project are excellent in developing CAD skills and are the best parts of the course.
boring, talks in hella monotone voice and makes u fall asleep in class. he made sure that any questions were answered though. but would come in late a lot

Professor Ahmadi mainly teaches theoretical stuff during lectures - don't expect to learn solidworks from him. His grading scheme is very forgiving, but the learning curve for this class can be high. He doesn't give very detailed rubric for the stapler. His lectures can also be boring, but he does give a lot of bonus points.

Amazing prof, always accessible. Dr Ahmadi always asked if we had any concerns before every single lecture. Make sure to attend office hours for any concerns. In-class activities and quizzes (10% each) dates are intentionally not shared to make students attend class. Final project takes alot of time, please don't wait till last minute.

Overall, not the worst prof. Could be better as the content was stuffed into lectures heavily. His monotone voice doesn't help how boring lectures are either. Though, you do learn a lot and he gives a lot of in-class practice.

Did not receive much feedback in this class, especially on assignments. The final project was intense and deadlines were not clear enough at all for something worth 20% of our grade. If you're struggling, definitely see him in his office hours and he's pretty helpful. Lectures were brutal (extremely boring) but I did learn a lot from this course.

Amazing professor. Cares a lot about his students, is always available for feedback or questions. Would highly recommend. Material is a bit simple, but he keeps his lectures fresh and interesting. 

One of my favourite professors I've ever had.
